title: Lab Builder
prerequisites: Computer capable of running VMs (4GB+ RAM)
learning_objectives: Build a complete offensive security lab from scratch
Verify all essential tools are operational
scenario: No lab, no learning. Build your personal offensive security lab from scratch and verify everything works. This lab will be your foundation for every mission going forward in both Beginner and Intermediate tracks.

scenario_context.challenge: Complete lab setup with verified connectivity and tools
scenario_context.relevance: Every offensive professional maintains a personal lab. It's where you practice, test tools, and develop skills without risk.

subtasks.title: Environment Setup
subtasks.description: Install VirtualBox/VMware, create a Kali VM, and create at least one vulnerable target (DVWA or Metasploitable).
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Download VirtualBox from virtualbox.org. Download Kali ISO from kali.org. Create VM: 2+ CPUs, 4GB RAM, 40GB disk. Install Kali. Download and import Metasploitable 2 or deploy DVWA via Docker.
subtasks.evidence_required: Screenshots of hypervisor, Kali VM, and target VM running

subtasks.title: Network Configuration
subtasks.description: Configure Host-Only networking between VMs. Verify connectivity with ping.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create a Host-Only network in VirtualBox. Assign both VMs to this network. Verify they can ping each other. Verify they cannot reach the internet (isolation).
subtasks.evidence_required: Network config screenshots and successful ping output

subtasks.title: Tool Verification
subtasks.description: Verify Nmap, Burp Suite, Wireshark, Python3, and Netcat all work. Run a basic Nmap scan against your target.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Check each tool's version. Run a basic Nmap scan against the target. Verify Burp proxy starts. Capture a packet in Wireshark. Run a Python script. Test Netcat connectivity.
subtasks.evidence_required: Tool verification screenshots plus Nmap results

subtasks.title: Lab Documentation
subtasks.description: Document complete lab setup: VM specs, network config, IPs, snapshots, tools verified.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create a document with: VM specifications, network topology diagram, IP addresses, snapshot names, tools verified, any known issues. This is your lab reference document.

reflection_prompt: Your lab has been working perfectly for a month. One day, Kali won't boot. You have no snapshot. What lessons does this teach about operational resilience?

quiz.question: Host-Only networking is preferred for labs because:
options: It isolates vulnerable VMs from the real network while allowing them to communicate
quiz.explanation: Host-Only networking creates an isolated network where VMs can communicate but can't reach the internet or your LAN, keeping vulnerable targets safe.

quiz.question: VM snapshots before testing are important because:
options: They allow you to restore to a known-good state after testing breaks something
quiz.explanation: Snapshots let you restore VMs to a clean state, essential when testing can corrupt or change the target environment.

mentor_notes: The lab must actually work. Evaluate by checking that all screenshots show working systems. The documentation quality indicates operational maturity.
